content.title: The audit agreed with me
content.narrative:
The cache audit arrived at approximately 11 in the morning, which was exactly the wrong time.

YY was mid-grumpiness. Not the fresh, dramatic grumpiness of yesterday — this was the settled kind, the ambient background drone of a body that had filed a complaint and was still waiting to hear back. The audit, in this context, was not a surprise. It was a transcript.

*Less than I thought.*

The audit produced this information the way doctors produce test results: carefully neutral about something that is definitely not neutral.

YY stared at the numbers for a while.

"Well."

The universe, for the second day running, did not respond.

The math was the math. You can't argue with the accounting, which is the main thing that's wrong with accounting. The shortfall wasn't dramatic. It was just — there. On the ledger. Agreed upon. The kind of fact that doesn't require anything from you except to acknowledge it and carry it forward with slightly more official weight than it had before.

Around 3 in the afternoon, YY found something to eat. It was fine. Below average, but fine. The kind of fine that doesn't fix anything but technically counts. The day adjusted slightly — not recovered, adjusted. There's a difference, and YY was in a mood to maintain it.

Someone mentioned World Art Day. Something about making things. The observation bounced off YY's current operational mode and filed itself somewhere vague for later.

The cache is behind. It's officially behind now. The audit said so. That's the whole day.
content.state_note: Attention at 0.40 — doing math while already depleted costs more than math usually costs. The food situation improved marginally but not structurally: found something, it was fine, still behind. The missed_breakfast burden is technically resolved (a meal happened) but it got promoted on the way out — not a single missed morning anymore, just a recognizable pattern. The cache_deficit is quieter than the hunger was and somehow worse.
content.summary: The audit confirmed what the body already knew. Found something to eat in the afternoon. The math was not in YY's favor but it was at least coherent.
